root_text.sn49.45-54:1.5: Imesaṁ kho, bhikkhave, pañcannaṁ uddhambhāgiyānaṁ saṁyojanānaṁ abhiññāya pariññāya parikkhayāya pahānāya cattāro sammappadhānā bhāvetabbā.
root_text.sn49.45-54:1.8: uppannānaṁ kusalānaṁ dhammānaṁ ṭhitiyā asammosāya bhiyyobhāvāya vepullāya bhāvanāya pāripūriyā chandaṁ janeti vāyamati vīriyaṁ ārabhati cittaṁ paggaṇhāti padahati.
root_text.sn49.45-54:1.9: Imesaṁ kho, bhikkhave, pañcannaṁ uddhambhāgiyānaṁ saṁyojanānaṁ abhiññāya pariññāya parikkhayāya pahānāya ime cattāro sammappadhānā bhāvetabbā”ti.

translation_text.sn49.45-54:1.3: Desire for rebirth in the realm of luminous form, desire for rebirth in the formless realm, conceit, restlessness, and ignorance.
translation_text.sn49.45-54:1.4: These are the five higher fetters.
translation_text.sn49.45-54:1.5: The four right efforts should be developed for the direct knowledge, complete understanding, finishing, and giving up of these five higher fetters.
translation_text.sn49.45-54:1.7: It’s when a mendicant generates enthusiasm, tries, makes an effort, exerts the mind, and strives so that bad, unskillful qualities don’t arise. …
translation_text.sn49.45-54:1.8: so that skillful qualities that have arisen remain, are not lost, but increase, mature, and are completed by development.
translation_text.sn49.45-54:1.9: These four right efforts should be developed for the direct knowledge, complete understanding, finishing, and giving up of these five higher fetters.”
translation_text.sn49.45-54:1.10: (Tell in full as in SN 45.171–179, with the above as the final discourse.)
translation_text.sn49.45-54:2.4: aggregates, and fetters high and low.
translation_text.sn49.45-54:2.5: The Linked Discourses on the Right Efforts is the fifth section.
